description: Three-state wallet connection control. In the disconnected / connecting states it renders a Button; so it inherits Button's focus, keyboard, and screen reader behavior. In the connected state it becomes a DropdownMenu whose trigger is a button containing the jazzicon, optional name, and truncated address; the menu hosts the disconnect action.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the control (single tab stop in every state).
In disconnected state, fires onConnect. In connected state, opens the menu. Inside the menu, activates the focused item.
Moves focus between menu items when the connected-state menu is open.
Closes the open menu and returns focus to the trigger.

focus.notes: Focus is visibly ringed on the trigger in every state. The menu uses Radix DropdownMenu's focus management; focus is scoped inside while open, restored to the trigger on close.

announcements: Disconnected button announces its label (default "Connect wallet").
Connecting button announces "Connecting…" and its disabled state, so assistive tech waits for the next change.
Connected trigger announces the name and address in reading order, then menu-open state.
screenReader.notes: The connecting label is plain text rather than an aria-live region, because the state transition is driven by user action; the user already knows it's happening. If your connector flow surfaces errors asynchronously, pair ConnectWallet with a separate toast / live region.

contrast.text: Uses Button's default tokens; meets WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Meets WCAG AA when used with the default theme tokens

motion.reducedMotion: The connecting spinner respects prefers-reduced-motion only if the consumer overrides it; the animate-spin utility is always on. Consider swapping to a static dots icon under reduced motion if that matters for your audience.

consumerResponsibilities: Own the status state; ConnectWallet is purely presentational. Derive status from wagmi / viem / your connector and pass it in.
Handle errors outside the component (e.g. user rejected the connect request). A toast or inline alert is the usual pattern.
If you expose multiple wallets (MetaMask, WalletConnect, Coinbase), wrap ConnectWallet in your own picker; the disconnected state is a single button by design.
